filter_index.video_title: The Trek That Starts at 16,000 Ft | Chandrabhaga Glacier Trek | Lahaul | Baralacha La | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow has thawed by late July except in bits/pieces along trail; mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso still snow-covered in July-August; lakes partly frozen with thin snow layer in July-August; by September-October most snow has melted except glacier ice; after mid-October snow sets in and region gets cut off
Lahaul lies in rain shadow of Pir Panjal range; no heavy rains like other parts of Himachal during monsoon/post-monsoon season
temperature_notes: July-August: Daytime 8-10°C; nights may drop to 0°C at campsites
September-October: Daytime 4-6°C; nights drop several degrees below zero at campsites; wind chill makes it feel much colder; 3 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Uneven trails with gradual ascents and descents
Boulder sections with scree and moraine
2.5 km long boulder section before Chandrabhaga Glaciers Viewpoint
Moraine ridges near glacial lakes Kya Tso and Ga Tso
Multiple stream crossings with glacial streams that can swell abruptly later in the day
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek starts at 16,000 ft and climbs to 17,200 ft, described as the highest on any Indiahikes trek
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) especially in the first couple of days
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; fitness does not eliminate risk if ascent is too fast
Heat exhaustion risk from direct sun exposure in open landscape at 16,000 ft
Strong winds near glaciers cause bitter cold and wind chill

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 32 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek.
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria of the trek.
Use apps like Nike and Strava to record runs; paste a public profile link on your Indiahikes account.
Fitness profile must be linked; failure to link or to meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month may lead to rejection.
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week plan to get trek-fit if not currently active

experience_themes: July-August: raw, rugged brown landscape with green patches near streams and snowmelt; snow in bits and pieces along trail (sometimes with icy base); streams full and fast from melting snow; mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso still snow-covered; lakes partly frozen with thin snow layer; wildflowers growing from rock cracks

September-October: classic cold desert with wide open brownish landscapes glowing golden, rust, or purple; loose boulders and pebbles on trail; easier stream crossings; clear blue skies and sharp views; jagged black mountain ridges; glaciers still hold ice; Kya Tso and Ga Tso fully thawed with turquoise blue colour; possible thin transparent ice sheet forming on lakes late in season
July-August: Sasher Kangri and Chandrabhaga ranges fully white with snow
September-October: best time for big mountain views; clear blue skies; sharp views; jagged black mountain ridges; glaciers still hold ice creating magical contrast; Kya Tso and Ga Tso turquoise blue

Snow has thawed by late July except in bits/pieces along trail; mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso still snow-covered in July-August; lakes partly frozen with thin snow layer in July-August; by September-October most snow has melted except glacier ice; after mid-October snow sets in and region gets cut off
primary_watchouts: July-August: snow base can be ice — tread carefully; streams full and fast from melting snow
September-October: trail over loose boulders and pebbles requires trained legs; coldest period with significant wind chill
After mid-October: temperatures drop sharply, water sources dry up, snow sets in and region gets cut off
Trekkers attempting after mid-October (region gets cut off by snow)
Trekkers unprepared for sub-zero nights and significant wind chill in September-October
Trekkers with legs untrained for loose boulder/pebble terrain in September-October
best_for: Trekkers seeking dramatic raw, rugged landscape with snow patches and wildflowers (July-August)
Trekkers seeking best big mountain views, clear skies, and turquoise glacial lakes (September-October)
Trekkers comfortable with high-altitude cold desert conditions and rain-shadow monsoon trekking
not_ideal_for: Trekkers attempting after mid-October (region gets cut off by snow)
Trekkers unprepared for sub-zero nights and significant wind chill in September-October
Trekkers with legs untrained for loose boulder/pebble terrain in September-October
